title: Plugin Lifecycle Flow

steps: CHECK arePluginsDisabled('local') → if false: getLocalRepository() → loadRepository(repo, isGlobal=false, rootPackage)
CHECK globalComposer !== null AND !arePluginsDisabled('global') → loadRepository(globalRepo, isGlobal=true)
BUILD weights: composer/installers or plugin-modifies-install-path → weight -10000
PackageSorter::sortPackages(packages, weights) → sorted by dependency order
SKIP if type not in [composer-plugin, composer-installer]
IF !isGlobalRepo AND package not in requiredPackages AND not isPluginAllowed(optional=true, prompt=false) → WARN + skip
FIND composer-plugin-api require link → if missing throw RuntimeException
CHECK api version constraint match → if mismatch WARN + return
CHECK isPluginAllowed(name, isGlobal, optional) → if not allowed: DEBUG log + return
CHECK registeredPlugins[name] already set → return (already registered)
READ extra['class'] → throw UnexpectedValueException if empty
BUILD installedRepo from localRepo + RootPackageRepository (files autoloads cleared)
FOREACH autoloadPackages: getInstallPath() → shouldAutoloadPackage(pkg, path) OR pkg===pluginPkg → add
FOREACH map['files']: composerRequire(fileId, file) [skip laminas bridge hack]
IF class_exists (already loaded): rename class with _composer_tmpN suffix via eval
VALIDATE implements InstallerInterface → throw if not
VALIDATE implements PluginInterface → throw if not
IF key in autoloadedPackages → return false (already processed, deduplication guard)
IF sourcePackage === null → trigger E_USER_DEPRECATED warning
ELSE CHECK isPluginAllowed(sourcePackage->getName(), ...) → DEBUG log + return if not allowed
io->writeError DEBUG: 'Loading plugin ClassName (from pkg, installed globally)'
array_search plugin in plugins[] → if not found return
CHECK registeredPlugins[name] exists → return if not
CHECK globalComposer !== null AND !arePluginsDisabled('global') → deactivateRepository(globalRepo, isGlobal=true)
FOREACH rules: Preg::isMatch(pattern, package) → return allow true/false
IF io->isInteractive() AND prompt → ask user y/n/d/?
y/n → persist to rules[] AND composer.json allow-plugins config → return bool
d → persist to rules[] only (discard from json) → return false
? → print help, increment attempts; after 5 attempts break

key_conditions: API version mismatch → skip with warning (no exception)
shouldAutoloadPackage: deduplication by name:version:path key, always loads the plugin package itself regardless
Class already loaded → eval rename trick with _composer_tmpN to avoid conflicts
loadRepository sorts by deps (required-by first), weights composer/installers to -10000 so it loads first
deactivateRepository uses reverse sort to unload dependents before dependencies
key_relationships: PluginManager depends_on Composer (getRepositoryManager, getAutoloadGenerator, getInstallationManager, getEventDispatcher, getConfig)
state_transitions: unregistered → registered: registerPackage() completes, plugin added to registeredPlugins[name]
registered+active → uninstalled: uninstallPackage() → removePlugin() + uninstallPlugin() → plugin->uninstall()
important_notes: Two plugin scopes: local (project) and global (~/.composer). Plugins can be individually disabled via disablePlugins='local'|'global'|true. allow-plugins config is security gate — missing/unlisted plugins either prompt user (interactive) or throw PluginBlockedException (non-interactive). Plugin class renaming via eval is used to avoid PHP class-already-defined errors when reloading. Root package files-autoload is intentionally cleared before plugin class loading to avoid loading files not yet available.
